new_classes.label_en: PGI Still Wine in Containers of More Than 2 Litres with Low Actual Alcoholic Strength (CN 22042980)
new_classes.label_de: g.g.A.-Stillwein in Behältnissen von mehr als 2 Litern mit niedrigem Alkoholgehalt (KN 22042980)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI/g.g.A.) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers of more than 2 litres, with a low actual alcoholic strength by volume, classified under CN code 22042980.

coverage_explanation: The legal text establishes two key criteria for CN 22042980: (1) the wine bears a Protected Geographical Indication (g.g.A./PGI) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, and (2) it is a still wine in containers of more than 2 litres. These are captured via subclass of PGIStillWineLargeContainer, the hasGeographicOriginQuality restriction to ProtectedGeographicalIndication, the container volume range restriction, and the codeString hasValue. The disjointness from the sibling 22042979 (high alcohol) is indicated via complement restriction. However, the specific alcoholic strength threshold that discriminates 22042980 (low) from 22042979 (high) is not precisely quantified in the available legal text note — the exact threshold referenced in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 is not reproduced here, so no precise numeric alcoholByVolumePercent upper bound can be axiomatised with certainty. This partial gap reduces coverage from 1.0.
